content:
# Disagreement Log -- Numbers 29 (NUM_029)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Date:** 2026-03-06

This log records all verses in Numbers 29 where the three translators diverged in their renderings, along with SOPHIA's resolution and reasoning.

---

## 29:1 -- "holy convocation shall be for you" vs. "you shall have a holy convocation" vs. "you shall have a sacred assembly"; "work of service" vs. "laborious work" vs. "ordinary work"; "a day of blowing" vs. "a day of trumpet-blowing" vs. "a day of trumpet blasts"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "a holy convocation shall be for you; any work of service you shall not do. A day of blowing it shall be for you." | Hebrew clause order preserved; "blowing" -- leaves instrument unspecified; "work of service" |
| LOGOS | "you shall have a holy convocation. You shall do no laborious work. It shall be a day of trumpet-blowing for you." | Smoothed English order; "trumpet-blowing" supplies instrument; "laborious work" |
| PNEUMA | "you shall have a sacred assembly; you shall do no ordinary work. It shall be a day of trumpet blasts for you." | "sacred assembly"; "ordinary work"; "trumpet blasts" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted EMET's Hebrew clause order ("a holy convocation shall be for you") and "any work of service you shall not do" per established conventions from Num 28. For *yom teru'ah*, adopted EMET's "a day of blowing" -- the Hebrew *teru'ah* does not specify an instrument (trumpet or shofar), so supplying "trumpet" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is interpretive. EMET's rendering preserves the Hebrew's ambiguity. Retained EMET's word order "A day of blowing it shall be for you" for the Hebrew predicate-first syntax.

**Status:** Resolved primarily in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:2 -- "son of the herd" presence/absence; "sons of a year" vs. "a year old" vs. "one year old"; word order of animal list; "offer" vs. "present" vs. "bring near"; "pleasing aroma" vs. "a scent that gives YHWH pleasure"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "you shall offer a burnt offering for a pleasing aroma to YHWH: one young bull, son of the herd, one ram, male lambs, sons of a year, seven, unblemished" | "offer"; "son of the herd"; "sons of a year, seven" -- number after noun; "pleasing aroma" |
| LOGOS | "you shall offer a burnt offering for a pleasing aroma to YHWH: one young bull, one ram, seven male lambs a year old without blemish" | "offer"; no "son of the herd"; "a year old"; number before noun; "without blemish" |
| PNEUMA | "you shall offer a burnt offering as a scent that gives YHWH pleasure: one young bull, one ram, and seven unblemished male lambs, one year old" | "offer"; "a scent that gives YHWH pleasure"; no "son of the herd"; "one year old"; reorders adjectives |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Used "present" (per established convention for hiphil of *qarav*). Retained "son of the herd" and "sons of a year" per Hebraism conventions. Placed "seven" before "male lambs" for English readability (departing from EMET's post-positioned number). Retained "pleasing aroma" (EMET/LOGOS) over PNEUMA's expansion. Used "unblemished" (EMET/PNEUMA) over "without blemish" (LOGOS).

**Status:** Resolved as hybrid with Hebraism-preservation priority.

---

## 29:4 -- "a tenth, one [tenth], for the one lamb" vs. "one-tenth for each lamb" vs. "one-tenth for each of the seven lambs"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "and a tenth, one [tenth], for the one lamb, for the seven lambs" | Preserves Hebrew distributive doubling; "the one lamb" |
| LOGOS | "and one-tenth for each lamb, for the seven lambs" | Smooths to single term; "each lamb" |
| PNEUMA | "and one-tenth for each of the seven lambs" | Combines into single phrase |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS's rendering "one-tenth for each lamb, for the seven lambs" as a compromise. The Hebrew here (*issaron issaron*) has a distributive sense. In this verse, unlike the parallel in 28:13/21/29, the Leningrad Codex reads *v'issaron echad* ("and a tenth, one") rather than the doubled *issaron issaron*. EMET's brackets acknowledge this difference. Since the Hebrew is *echad* ("one") not a true doubling, the LOGOS rendering is more accurate here. Retained "for the seven lambs" (EMET/LOGOS) as a separate distributive clause.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## 29:5 -- "sin offering" vs. "purification offering"; "upon you" vs. "for you" vs. "on your behalf"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "one male goat [for] a sin offering, to make atonement upon you" | "sin offering"; "upon you" -- literal *aleikhem* |
| LOGOS | "one male goat for a sin offering, to make atonement for you" | "sin offering"; "for you" |
| PNEUMA | "one male goat as a purification offering, to make atonement on your behalf" | "purification offering"; "on your behalf" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Retained "sin offering" (EMET/LOGOS) per established convention. Used "for you" (LOGOS) as the standard English rendering. PNEUMA's "purification offering" captures a legitimate semantic dimension of *khattat* but departs from the traditional rendering. PNEUMA's "on your behalf" over-interprets the simple preposition *al*.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## 29:6 -- "the burnt offering of the month" vs. "the burnt offering of the new moon" vs. "the monthly burnt offering"; "according to their ordinance" vs. "according to what is prescribed for them"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "apart from the burnt offering of the month and its grain offering... according to their ordinance" | "the month"; "according to their ordinance" |
| LOGOS | "besides the burnt offering of the new moon and its grain offering... according to their ordinance" | "the new moon"; "according to their ordinance" |
| PNEUMA | "in addition to the monthly burnt offering and its grain offering... according to what is prescribed for them" | "the monthly burnt offering"; "according to what is prescribed for them" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted EMET's "the burnt offering of the month" as the most literal rendering of *olat ha-chodesh*. The Hebrew *chodesh* means "month" (EMET) or "new moon" (LOGOS) -- both are valid, but "month" is more literal. PNEUMA's adjectival "monthly" restructures the Hebrew genitive. Used "apart from" (EMET) per convention. Used "according to their ordinance" (EMET/LOGOS) over PNEUMA's paraphrase.

**Status:** Resolved primarily in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:7 -- "a holy convocation shall be for you" vs. "you shall have a holy convocation" vs. "you shall have a sacred assembly"; "afflict your souls" vs. "afflict yourselves"; "any work" vs. "no manner of work"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "a holy convocation shall be for you, and you shall afflict your souls; any work you shall not do" | Hebrew clause order; "afflict your souls"; "any work you shall not do" |
| LOGOS | "you shall have a holy convocation, and you shall afflict your souls. You shall do no manner of work." | Smoothed English; "afflict your souls"; "no manner of work" |
| PNEUMA | "you shall have a sacred assembly. And you shall afflict yourselves; you shall do no manner of work." | "sacred assembly"; "afflict yourselves"; "no manner of work" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Retained EMET's Hebrew clause order ("a holy convocation shall be for you"). "Afflict your souls" (EMET/LOGOS) preserved over PNEUMA's "afflict yourselves" -- the Hebrew explicitly says *et-nafshoteikhem* ("your souls"), and this Hebraism should be preserved. For the work prohibition, the Hebrew here is *kol-melakhah* (not *melekhet avodah*), reflecting the stricter Yom Kippur prohibition. Used EMET's "any work you shall not do" to preserve Hebrew word order and the distinction from "work of service" in other festival verses.

**Status:** Resolved primarily in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:8 -- "offer" vs. "present" vs. "bring near"; "a pleasing aroma" placement; "son of the herd" presence/absence

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "you shall offer a burnt offering to YHWH, a pleasing aroma: one young bull, son of the herd, one ram, male lambs, sons of a year, seven; unblemished they shall be for you" | "offer"; "a pleasing aroma" in apposition; "son of the herd"; number after noun |
| LOGOS | "you shall present a burnt offering to YHWH for a pleasing aroma: one young bull, one ram, seven male lambs a year old -- they shall be without blemish for you" | "present"; "for a pleasing aroma"; no "son of the herd"; "a year old"; "without blemish" |
| PNEUMA | "you shall bring near a burnt offering to YHWH as a scent that gives him pleasure: one young bull, one ram, and seven male lambs one year old -- they shall be unblemished for you" | "bring near"; PNEUMA's aroma phrase with "him"; no "son of the herd"; "one year old" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Used "present" per convention. Retained "son of the herd" and "sons of a year" per Hebraism conventions. The Hebrew reads *olah la-YHWH re'ach nikhoach*, with "a pleasing aroma" in apposition -- adopted EMET's punctuation with comma. Placed "seven" before "male lambs" for readability. Used "unblemished" (EMET/PNEUMA).

**Status:** Resolved as hybrid with Hebraism-preservation priority.

---

## 29:10 -- "a tenth, a tenth" vs. "a tenth for each lamb" vs. "a tenth for each of the seven lambs"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "a tenth, a tenth [of an ephah] for the one lamb, for the seven lambs" | Preserves Hebrew doubling *issaron issaron*; "the one lamb" |
| LOGOS | "a tenth for each lamb, for the seven lambs" | Smooths doubling; "each lamb" |
| PNEUMA | "a tenth for each of the seven lambs" | Combines into single phrase |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Retained EMET's doubled "a tenth, a tenth of an ephah" per the established convention of preserving Hebrew distributive constructions. Used "each lamb" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) for clarity in the distributive context. Retained "for the seven lambs" (EMET/LOGOS).

**Status:** Resolved as hybrid: EMET's doubling, LOGOS's "each lamb."

---

## 29:11 -- "sin offering" vs. "purification offering"; "apart from" vs. "besides" vs. "in addition to"; "sin offering of atonement" vs. "purification offering of atonement"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "one male goat [for] a sin offering; apart from the sin offering of atonement, and the continual burnt offering, and its grain offering, and their drink offerings" | "sin offering of atonement"; "apart from" |
| LOGOS | "one male goat for a sin offering, besides the sin offering of atonement and the continual burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings" | "sin offering of atonement"; "besides" |
| PNEUMA | "one male goat as a purification offering, in addition to the purification offering of atonement, and the regular burnt offering and its grain offering, together with their drink offerings" | "purification offering of atonement"; "in addition to"; "regular" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Retained "sin offering" and "sin offering of atonement" (EMET/LOGOS) per convention. Used "apart from" (EMET) for *mil-bad*. Retained "continual" (EMET/LOGOS) over "regular" (PNEUMA).

**Status:** Resolved primarily in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:12 -- "holy convocation" vs. "sacred assembly"; "work of service" vs. "laborious work" vs. "ordinary work"; "a feast" vs. "a festival"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "a holy convocation shall be for you; any work of service you shall not do. And you shall celebrate a feast to YHWH seven days." | Hebrew clause order; "work of service"; "a feast" |
| LOGOS | "you shall have a holy convocation. You shall do no laborious work, and you shall celebrate a feast to YHWH seven days." | Smoothed order; "laborious work"; "a feast" |
| PNEUMA | "you shall have a sacred assembly; you shall do no ordinary work. And you shall celebrate a festival to YHWH for seven days." | "sacred assembly"; "ordinary work"; "a festival"; "for seven days" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Retained EMET's Hebrew clause order and "work of service" per convention. Retained "holy convocation" (EMET/LOGOS). Used "a feast" (EMET/LOGOS) over "a festival" (PNEUMA) per Num 28:17 precedent. Used "seven days" (EMET/LOGOS) without the preposition "for" (PNEUMA).

**Status:** Resolved primarily in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:13 -- "offer" vs. "present" vs. "bring near"; "sons of the herd" presence/absence; "sons of a year" vs. "a year old" vs. "one year old"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "you shall offer a burnt offering, a fire-offering of a pleasing aroma to YHWH: thirteen young bulls, sons of the herd, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year; unblemished they shall be" | "offer"; "sons of the herd"; "sons of a year"; "pleasing aroma" |
| LOGOS | "you shall present a burnt offering, a fire offering of a pleasing aroma to YHWH: thirteen young bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old; they shall be without blemish" | "present"; no "sons of the herd"; "a year old"; "without blemish" |
| PNEUMA | "you shall bring near a burnt offering, a fire-offering as a scent that gives YHWH pleasure: thirteen young bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs one year old -- they shall be unblemished" | "bring near"; "a scent that gives YHWH pleasure"; "one year old" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Used "present" per convention. Retained "sons of the herd" and "sons of a year" per Hebraism conventions. Used "pleasing aroma" (EMET/LOGOS). Used "unblemished they shall be" (EMET's word order).

**Status:** Resolved as hybrid with Hebraism-preservation priority.

---

## 29:14 -- "for the one bull, for the thirteen bulls" vs. "for each of the thirteen bulls"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "three-tenths [of an ephah] for the one bull, for the thirteen bulls, two-tenths for the one ram, for the two rams" | Preserves Hebrew distributive *la-par ha-echad*; then total count |
| LOGOS | "three-tenths of an ephah for each of the thirteen bulls, two-tenths for each of the two rams" | Smooths to distributive "each of" |
| PNEUMA | "three-tenths of an ephah for each of the thirteen bulls, two-tenths for each of the two rams" | Same as LOGOS |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS/PNEUMA's "each of the thirteen bulls" and "each of the two rams" since the Hebrew distributive construction *la-par ha-echad li-shloshah asar parim* is best rendered in English with "each of." EMET's literal rendering, while faithful to word order, is cumbersome in English. This follows the precedent set in Num 28:12 and 28:28.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS/PNEUMA.

---

## 29:15 -- "a tenth, a tenth" vs. "a tenth for each lamb"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "a tenth, a tenth [of an ephah] for the one lamb, for the fourteen lambs" | Preserves Hebrew doubling; "the one lamb" |
| LOGOS | "a tenth for each lamb, for the fourteen lambs" | Smooths; "each lamb" |
| PNEUMA | "a tenth for each of the fourteen lambs" | Combines into single phrase |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Retained EMET's doubled "a tenth, a tenth of an ephah" per convention. Used "each lamb" for clarity. Retained "for the fourteen lambs" (EMET/LOGOS).

**Status:** Resolved as hybrid: EMET's doubling, LOGOS's "each lamb."

---

## 29:16 -- "sin offering" vs. "purification offering"; "apart from" vs. "besides" vs. "in addition to"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "one male goat [for] a sin offering; apart from the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering" | "sin offering"; "apart from" |
| LOGOS | "one male goat for a sin offering, besides the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering" | "sin offering"; "besides" |
| PNEUMA | "one male goat as a purification offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering" | "purification offering"; "in addition to"; "regular" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Retained "sin offering" (EMET/LOGOS), "apart from" (EMET), and "continual" (EMET/LOGOS) per established conventions.

**Status:** Resolved primarily in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:17 -- "sons of the herd" presence/absence; "sons of a year" vs. "a year old" vs. "one year old"; conjunction "and" before "fourteen"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "twelve young bulls, sons of the herd, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished" | "sons of the herd"; "sons of a year"; no "and" before fourteen |
| LOGOS | "twelve young bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish" | No "sons of the herd"; "a year old"; "without blemish" |
| PNEUMA | "twelve young bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished male lambs one year old" | No "sons of the herd"; "and" before fourteen; "one year old"; reorders "unblemished" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Retained "sons of the herd" and "sons of a year" per Hebraism conventions. Used "unblemished" (EMET/PNEUMA). The Hebrew text of v. 17 does include *bnei baqar* ("sons of the herd"), though LOGOS and PNEUMA omit it. EMET's inclusion is correct and consistent with the source.

**Status:** Resolved primarily in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:18 -- "by their number, according to the ordinance" vs. "according to their number, as prescribed"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "by their number, according to the ordinance" | "by their number"; "according to the ordinance" |
| LOGOS | "by their number, according to the ordinance" | Same as EMET |
| PNEUMA | "according to their number, as prescribed" | "according to their number"; "as prescribed" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted EMET/LOGOS's rendering. "By their number" is more concise. "According to the ordinance" renders *ka-mishpatam* more fully than PNEUMA's "as prescribed."

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET/LOGOS.

---

## 29:19 -- "apart from" vs. "besides" vs. "in addition to"; "sin offering" vs. "purification offering"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "one male goat [for] a sin offering; apart from the continual burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings" | "sin offering"; "apart from" |
| LOGOS | "one male goat for a sin offering, besides the continual burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings" | "sin offering"; "besides" |
| PNEUMA | "one male goat as a purification offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering, together with their drink offerings" | "purification offering"; "in addition to"; "regular"; "together with" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Per established conventions: "sin offering," "apart from," "continual." PNEUMA's "together with" for the drink offerings adds a conjunction not present in the Hebrew.

**Status:** Resolved primarily in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:20 -- "sons of a year" vs. "a year old" vs. "one year old"; conjunction "and" before "fourteen"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "eleven bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished" | "sons of a year"; no "and" before fourteen |
| LOGOS | "eleven bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish" | "a year old"; "without blemish" |
| PNEUMA | "eleven bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished male lambs one year old" | "and" before fourteen; "one year old"; reorders adjectives |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Retained "sons of a year" and "unblemished" per convention. Note: from v. 20 onward the Hebrew abbreviates to *parim* ("bulls") without *bnei baqar* ("sons of the herd"), correctly reflected by all three translators.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:22 -- "apart from" vs. "besides" vs. "in addition to"; "sin offering" vs. "purification offering"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "one male goat [for] a sin offering; apart from the continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering" | Standard EMET pattern |
| LOGOS | "one male goat for a sin offering, besides the continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering" | "besides" |
| PNEUMA | "one male goat as a purification offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering" | "purification offering"; "in addition to"; "regular" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Per established conventions. Identical pattern to vv. 16, 19.

**Status:** Resolved primarily in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:23 -- Same pattern as v. 20 (age/blemish terminology)

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "ten bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished" | "sons of a year" |
| LOGOS | "ten bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish" | "a year old"; "without blemish" |
| PNEUMA | "ten bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished male lambs one year old" | "one year old"; reorders adjectives |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Per established conventions. "Sons of a year, unblemished."

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:25 -- "apart from" vs. "besides" vs. "in addition to"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "apart from the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering" | "apart from" |
| LOGOS | "besides the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering" | "besides" |
| PNEUMA | "in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering" | "in addition to"; "regular" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Per established conventions. "Apart from the continual burnt offering."

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:26 -- Same pattern as vv. 20, 23

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "nine bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished" | "sons of a year" |
| LOGOS | "nine bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish" | "a year old"; "without blemish" |
| PNEUMA | "nine bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished male lambs one year old" | "one year old"; reorders adjectives |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Per established conventions.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:28 -- Same pattern as vv. 19, 22

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "apart from the continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering" | "apart from" |
| LOGOS | "besides the continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering" | "besides" |
| PNEUMA | "in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering" | "in addition to"; "regular" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Per established conventions.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:29 -- Same pattern as vv. 20, 23, 26

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "eight bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished" | "sons of a year" |
| LOGOS | "eight bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish" | "a year old"; "without blemish" |
| PNEUMA | "eight bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished male lambs one year old" | "one year old"; reorders adjectives |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Per established conventions.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:31 -- "apart from" vs. "besides" vs. "in addition to"; singular vs. plural "drink offerings"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "apart from the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offerings" | "apart from"; plural "drink offerings" |
| LOGOS | "besides the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offerings" | "besides"; plural "drink offerings" |
| PNEUMA | "in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offerings" | "in addition to"; "regular"; plural "drink offerings" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Per established conventions. All three agree on plural "drink offerings" here (Hebrew *v'niskeha* can be read as plural), which is retained.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET for preposition and "continual."

---

## 29:32 -- Same pattern as vv. 20, 23, 26, 29

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "seven bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished" | "sons of a year" |
| LOGOS | "seven bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish" | "a year old"; "without blemish" |
| PNEUMA | "seven bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished male lambs one year old" | "one year old"; reorders adjectives |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Per established conventions.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:34 -- "apart from" vs. "besides" vs. "in addition to"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "apart from the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering" | "apart from" |
| LOGOS | "besides the continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering" | "besides" |
| PNEUMA | "in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering" | "in addition to"; "regular" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Per established conventions.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:35 -- "an assembly" vs. "a solemn assembly"; "work of service" vs. "laborious work" vs. "ordinary work"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "On the eighth day, an assembly shall be for you; any work of service you shall not do." | "an assembly" -- bare *atzeret*; Hebrew clause order |
| LOGOS | "On the eighth day you shall have a solemn assembly. You shall do no laborious work." | "a solemn assembly"; smoothed English order |
| PNEUMA | "On the eighth day you shall have a solemn assembly; you shall do no ordinary work." | "a solemn assembly"; "ordinary work" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted EMET's "an assembly" -- the Hebrew *atzeret* is a single word meaning "assembly" or "closing gathering." The adjective "solemn" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is an English interpretive addition not present in the Hebrew. Retained EMET's clause order ("an assembly shall be for you") and "any work of service you shall not do." Note: the work prohibition here returns to *melekhet avodah* ("work of service"), distinct from the stricter *kol-melakhah* ("any work") of v. 7 (Yom Kippur).

**Status:** Resolved primarily in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:36 -- "offer" vs. "present" vs. "bring near"; "sons of a year" vs. "a year old" vs. "one year old"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "you shall offer a burnt offering, a fire-offering of a pleasing aroma to YHWH: one bull, one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished" | "offer"; "sons of a year"; "pleasing aroma" |
| LOGOS | "you shall present a burnt offering, a fire offering of a pleasing aroma to YHWH: one bull, one ram, seven male lambs a year old without blemish" | "present"; "a year old"; "without blemish" |
| PNEUMA | "you shall bring near a burnt offering, a fire-offering as a scent that gives YHWH pleasure: one bull, one ram, and seven unblemished male lambs one year old" | "bring near"; PNEUMA's aroma phrase; "one year old"; reorders adjectives |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Used "present" per convention. Retained "sons of a year," "unblemished," and "pleasing aroma" per conventions.

**Status:** Resolved as hybrid with Hebraism-preservation priority.

---

## 29:38 -- "apart from" vs. "besides" vs. "in addition to"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "apart from the continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering" | "apart from" |
| LOGOS | "besides the continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering" | "besides" |
| PNEUMA | "in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering" | "in addition to"; "regular" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Per established conventions.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:39 -- "appointed times" vs. "appointed feasts" vs. "appointed festivals"; "apart from" vs. "besides" vs. "in addition to"; sentence structure

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "These you shall offer to YHWH at your appointed times, apart from your vows and your freewill offerings, for your burnt offerings and for your grain offerings and for your drink offerings and for your peace offerings." | "appointed times"; "apart from"; straightforward listing |
| LOGOS | "These you shall offer to YHWH at your appointed feasts, besides your vows and your freewill offerings, for your burnt offerings and for your grain offerings and for your drink offerings and for your peace offerings." | "appointed feasts"; "besides" |
| PNEUMA | "These you shall offer to YHWH at your appointed festivals -- in addition to your vow offerings and your freewill offerings -- whether burnt offerings, grain offerings, drink offerings, or peace offerings." | "appointed festivals"; "in addition to"; parenthetical dashes; "vow offerings"; "whether... or" restructuring |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted EMET's "appointed times" as the most literal rendering of *mo'adim* (the term encompasses more than just feasts/festivals). Used "apart from" per convention. Retained EMET/LOGOS's straightforward listing structure over PNEUMA's restructuring with "whether... or." Used "vows" (EMET/LOGOS) over PNEUMA's "vow offerings" -- the Hebrew *nedarim* means "vows," not "vow offerings."

**Status:** Resolved primarily in favor of EMET.

---

## 29:40 -- "spoke" vs. "told"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "And Moses spoke to the sons of Israel according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses." | "spoke" -- *vayyomer* |
| LOGOS | "And Moses told the sons of Israel according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses." | "told" -- interprets *vayyomer* as communicative act directed at audience |
| PNEUMA | "And Moses spoke to the sons of Israel according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses." | "spoke" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted "spoke" (EMET/PNEUMA) as the standard rendering of *vayyomer* ("and he said/spoke"). LOGOS's "told" is semantically close but introduces a slightly different nuance (implying directed instruction rather than speech). The Hebrew *vayyomer... el* is most naturally "spoke to."

**Status:** Resolved in favor of EMET/PNEUMA.

---

## Summary of Divergence Patterns

**Systematic divergences recurring throughout this chapter:**

1. **"Pleasing aroma" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "a scent that gives YHWH pleasure" (PNEUMA)** -- appears in vv. 2, 6, 8, 13, 36. Resolved consistently in favor of EMET/LOGOS.

2. **"Sons of a year" (EMET) vs. "a year old" (LOGOS) vs. "one year old" (PNEUMA)** -- appears in vv. 2, 8, 13, 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 36. Resolved consistently in favor of EMET per Hebraism convention.

3. **"Continual" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "regular" (PNEUMA)** for *tamid* -- appears in vv. 6, 11, 16, 19, 22, 25, 28, 31, 34, 38. Resolved consistently in favor of EMET/LOGOS.

4. **"Sin offering" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "purification offering" (PNEUMA)** for *khattat* -- appears in vv. 5, 11, 16, 19, 22, 25, 28, 31, 34, 38. Resolved consistently in favor of EMET/LOGOS.

5. **"Holy convocation" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "sacred assembly" (PNEUMA)** for *miqra qodesh* -- appears in vv. 1, 7, 12. Resolved in favor of EMET/LOGOS.

6. **"Any work of service" (EMET) vs. "laborious work" (LOGOS) vs. "ordinary work" (PNEUMA)** for *melekhet avodah* -- appears in vv. 1, 12, 35. Resolved in favor of EMET.

7. **"Apart from" (EMET) vs. "besides" (LOGOS) vs. "in addition to" (PNEUMA)** for *mil-bad* -- appears in vv. 6, 11, 16, 19, 22, 25, 28, 31, 34, 38, 39. Resolved consistently in favor of EMET.

8. **"Sons of the herd" (EMET) vs. omitted (LOGOS/PNEUMA)** -- appears in vv. 2, 8, 13, 17. Resolved in favor of EMET per Hebraism convention.

9. **Distributive doubling "a tenth, a tenth" (EMET) vs. smoothed (LOGOS/PNEUMA)** -- appears in vv. 10, 15. Resolved in favor of EMET.

10. **"Present" (LOGOS) vs. "offer" (EMET) vs. "bring near" (PNEUMA)** for hiphil of *qarav* -- appears in vv. 2, 8, 13, 36. Resolved in favor of LOGOS's "present."

11. **"Unblemished" (EMET/PNEUMA) vs. "without blemish" (LOGOS)** -- appears throughout. Resolved in favor of EMET/PNEUMA.

**Unique divergences in this chapter:**

12. **"A day of blowing" (EMET) vs. "a day of trumpet-blowing" (LOGOS) vs. "a day of trumpet blasts" (PNEUMA)** in v. 1 -- resolved in favor of EMET (instrument unspecified in Hebrew).

13. **"Afflict your souls" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "afflict yourselves" (PNEUMA)** in v. 7 -- resolved in favor of EMET/LOGOS (Hebrew explicitly says *nafshoteikhem*, "your souls").

14. **"An assembly" (EMET) vs. "a solemn assembly" (LOGOS/PNEUMA)** in v. 35 -- resolved in favor of EMET ("solemn" is an English addition).

15. **"Spoke" (EMET/PNEUMA) vs. "told" (LOGOS)** in v. 40 -- resolved in favor of EMET/PNEUMA.

**Verses with no meaningful divergence:** Verses 3, 9, 18 (EMET/LOGOS identical), 21, 24, 27, 30, 33, 37 (formulaic Sukkot verses where all three translators produced essentially the same rendering apart from the systematic divergences listed above).

-- SOPHIA
